channel.title: VMware Cloud Foundation on My Cloudy World
channel.description: Recent content in VMware Cloud Foundation on My Cloudy World
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2019–2025, Gary Blake; all rights reserved.

item.title: Configuring VCF Single Sign-On: Using OpenLDAP Identity Provider
item.description:
VMware Cloud Foundation 9.0 introduced the new identity broker capabilities where you can configure VCF Single Sign-On across VCF components within a single VCF Instance, across a set of VCF Instances, and/or across an entire VCF fleet of VCF Instances. This post walks through the process of configuring OpenLDAP as an identity provider.

Procedure - Configure an Identity Provider

  1. Log in to the VCF Operations console at https://<vcf_operations_fqdn> with a user assigned the Administrator role.

item.title: Native PowerCLI Modules for VMware Cloud Foundation
item.description:
The Birth of PowerVCF

Back in January 2020 my friend and colleague Brian OConnell embarked on a community project to write a PowerShell module to make consumption of the VMware Cloud Foundation APIs simple and easy to consume by VI Admins, each cmdlet was hand-built using standard PowerShell verbs that the community is well versed in consuming. He saw that the need for automation within our customer based was growing and at the time there was no alternative solution available.
